bottlenecks.description: Build system instability - multiple commits fixing Nuitka, electron-builder, Windows-specific issues
bottlenecks.frequency: 12+ fix commits related to build issues

bottlenecks.description: Windows build challenges with DLL dependencies and icon handling
CI pipeline required multiple iterations to get right (parse errors, encoding issues)

branchPatterns.mergeStrategy: Direct commits to main, no merge commits visible
branchPatterns.namingConvention: Direct commits to main (no feature branches detected in recent history)

commitPatterns.averageFrequency: 29 commits over project lifetime (~3 months), approximately 1-2 commits per week
commitPatterns.messageFormat: Conventional commits style (ci:, fix:, feat:, docs:, test:, Translate:)

majorEvents.event: Project structure created with German documentation
CI/CD pipeline setup with GitHub Actions

technologyChanges: Started with basic Electron + Python setup
Migrated from Dependency Walker to lucasg/Dependencies for Windows builds

implicitRules: All documentation should be in English (migrated from German)
Use conventional commit prefixes (ci:, fix:, feat:, docs:)
Build system changes require testing on all platforms
Python backend uses German comments but English function names

painPoints.description: Complex build process with Nuitka compilation and multi-platform electron-builder
Windows builds require special handling (Dependencies, DLL management)
CI pipeline required multiple rewrites to stabilize

releasePatterns.tagFormat: No tags found - no release tagging convention established yet
releasePatterns.versioningScheme: Semantic versioning implied by package.json (0.1.0), but not enforced via tags
